user.title: Solar Load Manager
user.description: Point the manager at your PV surplus and dynamic price sensors. Devices are added later via the Configure menu of this entry.

data_description.hourly_balance_sensor: Net production minus consumption accumulated in the current hour, in kWh (resets each hour). Positive = net exporter so far. This drives both the power budget and which price applies.
data_description.sell_price_sensor: What one exported kWh earns you (e.g. RCE PSE Price). Used as the marginal cost while the hour's balance is positive - consuming then only forgoes this revenue.
data_description.buy_price_sensor: Your time-of-day import tariff. Used as the marginal cost once the hour's balance goes negative.
data_description.buy_price_attribute: If the tariff sensor's state is not numeric, read the price from this attribute (default: 'price').
data_description.smoothing_seconds: Smoothing window for the net power derived from the hourly balance. Larger = calmer but slower reactions. Default 300 s.
data_description.import_tolerance: How many watts of grid import to tolerate before shedding loads. Also absorbs estimation error in consumption. Default 300 W.
data_description.cheap_price: When the marginal price (sell while exporting, tariff while importing) is at or below this, devices run regardless of the power budget. Default 0.15.
data_description.override_minutes: When you change a managed device by hand, the manager leaves it alone for this long. Default 30 min.

hub.title: Hub settings
hub.description: Sensors and global thresholds shared by all managed devices.

data_description.hourly_balance_sensor: Net production minus consumption accumulated in the current hour, in kWh (resets each hour). Positive = net exporter so far. This drives both the power budget and which price applies.
data_description.sell_price_sensor: What one exported kWh earns you (e.g. RCE PSE Price). Used as the marginal cost while the hour's balance is positive - consuming then only forgoes this revenue.
data_description.buy_price_sensor: Your time-of-day import tariff. Used as the marginal cost once the hour's balance goes negative.
data_description.buy_price_attribute: If the tariff sensor's state is not numeric, read the price from this attribute (default: 'price').
data_description.smoothing_seconds: Smoothing window for the net power derived from the hourly balance. Larger = calmer but slower reactions. Default 300 s.
data_description.import_tolerance: How many watts of grid import to tolerate before shedding loads. Also absorbs estimation error in consumption. Default 300 W.
data_description.cheap_price: When the marginal price (sell while exporting, tariff while importing) is at or below this, devices run regardless of the power budget. Default 0.15.
data_description.override_minutes: When you change a managed device by hand, the manager leaves it alone for this long. Default 30 min.

add_device.title: Add managed device
add_device.description: Step 1 of 2: name, priority and safety limits. The next step asks what to control.

data_description.name: Shown in entity names and used in the boost service. Renaming later creates new entities.
data_description.priority: 1 = served first. When surplus shrinks, the highest number is shed first.
data_description.device_type: switch: anything with on/off. climate: thermostat controlled via HVAC mode. tesla: EV charging with amp modulation.
data_description.min_on_minutes: Once started, the device keeps running at least this long - protects compressors and heaters from short cycling.
data_description.min_off_minutes: Once stopped, the device stays off at least this long before the manager may restart it.
data_description.must_run_enabled: Force the device on daily within the window below, regardless of sun and price - a comfort guarantee.
data_description.must_run_start: Window start (local time). A window crossing midnight is supported.
data_description.max_price: The device will not start when the current marginal price (RCE while net-exporting, tariff while importing) exceeds this. 999 = no limit. Example: 0.3 lets the hot tub run on surplus or off-peak, never at peak tariff.

edit_device.title: Edit managed device
edit_device.description: Pick a device, then adjust its settings in the following steps.

data_description.priority: 1 = served first. When surplus shrinks, the highest number is shed first.
data_description.device_type: switch: anything with on/off. climate: thermostat controlled via HVAC mode. tesla: EV charging with amp modulation.
data_description.min_on_minutes: Once started, the device keeps running at least this long - protects compressors and heaters from short cycling.
data_description.min_off_minutes: Once stopped, the device stays off at least this long before the manager may restart it.
data_description.must_run_enabled: Force the device on daily within the window below, regardless of sun and price - a comfort guarantee.
data_description.must_run_start: Window start (local time). A window crossing midnight is supported.
data_description.max_price: The device will not start when the current marginal price (RCE while net-exporting, tariff while importing) exceeds this. 999 = no limit. Example: 0.3 lets the hot tub run on surplus or off-peak, never at peak tariff.

onoff.title: Controlled device
onoff.description: Step 2 of 2: which entity to switch and how much power it draws.

data_description.entity: The switch or climate entity the manager turns on and off.
data_description.rated_power: Typical power draw while running. Check the nameplate or watch your consumption jump when it starts. The manager reserves this much surplus for it.
data_description.on_factor: Safety margin for starting: with 1.1 the device starts only when the surplus covers 110% of its rated power. Keeping on requires just 100%, which gives hysteresis.
data_description.hvac_mode: Mode set when a climate device is turned on (heat for jacuzzi/boiler, cool for AC). Ignored for switches.

tesla.title: Tesla charging settings
tesla.description: Step 2 of 2: charging entities from your Tesla integration. Amps are recalculated every minute: available watts / (voltage x phases).

data_description.charge_switch: Switch that starts/stops charging, e.g. switch.charge. If the car stops by itself (limit reached), the manager backs off instead of fighting it.
data_description.current_number: Number entity that sets the charging current, e.g. number.charge_current.
data_description.cable_sensor: Binary sensor that is on while the cable is plugged in, e.g. binary_sensor.charge_cable. Nothing happens when disconnected.
data_description.charger_power_sensor: Current charging power in kW, e.g. sensor.charger_power. Needed so the car's own draw counts back into the available budget.
data_description.phases: 3 for a three-phase wall connector (16 A = 11 kW), 1 for single-phase (16 A = 3.7 kW). Verify: charging power = amps x 230 x phases.
data_description.min_amps: Below this the charger is inefficient, so charging stops instead. Tesla minimum is 5 A.
data_description.max_amps: Upper limit - your wall connector / circuit rating, typically 16 A.

remove_device.title: Remove managed device
remove_device.description: Removes the device from management and deletes its enable switch and status sensor. The physical device itself is not touched.

error.name_exists: A device with this name already exists.
